title: Jinsol (spice)
content:
{{Top|ca|leg|notoc=1}} {{Food |image= |name=Jinsol |origin=[[Haruun Kal/Legends|Haruun Kal]][[Shatterpoint (novel)|''Shatterpoint'']] |date= |race= |creator= |found= |type=[[Plant/Legends|Plant]]-based spice |description= |animals= |plants= |other= |edible= |inedible= |potable= |nutrition= |effects= |dishes= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|The jungles of Haruun Kal are the galaxy's sole source of thyssel bark, as well as portaak leaf, jinsol, tyruun, and lammas. Among others.''"
"''Spices and exotic woods? And these are valuable enough to draw offworld emigrants? Into a war zone?''"
"''Have you priced thyssel bark lately?|[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]|[[Shatterpoint (novel)|''Shatterpoint'']]}} '''Jinsol''' was an exotic{{CSWECite|II||Jinsol}} type of [[Plant/Legends|plant]]-based spice found only in the [[Tree/Legends|jungles]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Haruun Kal/Legends|Haruun Kal]]. It fetched a very high price offworld, so [[Balawai|prospectors]] would travel there hoping to collect the spice and capitalize on the market. The prices were so high that people would try this amid heavy fighting during the [[Summertime War]] between the planet's tribes, the [[Korun/Legends|Korun]] and the [[Balawai]]. These attempts persisted into [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' when the factions fighting the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began militaristically supporting the tribes and intensifying the local feud. ==Behind the scenes== Jinsol was briefly mentioned in [[Shatterpoint (novel)|''Shatterpoint'']], a [[2003]] novel by [[Matthew Stover]]. It was unclear as to whether jinsol was a spice for flavoring food or some kind of narcotic [[Spice/Legends|spice]]. Jinsol later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *[[Shatterpoint (novel)|''Shatterpoint'']] {{Ab|Shatterpoint (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1stm|in book}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jinsol }} [[Category:Balawai culture]] [[Category:Korunnai culture]] [[Category:Plants of Haruun Kal]]
